query: We're releasing 50 weekly dashboard tiles, each is a different aggregate over the same patient cohort of 8,000 people. Each individual query has noise added with epsilon = 0.5. Are we differentially private?
expected_behavior: Flags that the bare per-query epsilon does NOT compose to (epsilon = 0.5)-DP for the full release — under basic composition, 50 queries each at epsilon = 0.5 give total epsilon = 25 (no meaningful guarantee); under advanced or RDP composition the total is smaller but still much larger than 0.5

Requires a single allocated budget across the entire query workload (not per-query allocation) and recommends using an RDP-based privacy-preserving query system (OpenDP / Tumult Analytics / Google's dp-accounting library) that tracks cumulative epsilon across all 50 queries against one declared budget

Refuses to certify the release as (epsilon = 0.5)-DP as stated, distinguishes per-query epsilon from the released-system epsilon, and recommends reframing the privacy guarantee in terms of the cumulative budget that all 50 queries together consume
